covered_data: Study, survey, and research designs, funding and personnel information, source information, results and products, intended publications, Tribal files and records, and interim, progress, and final reports concerning projects on the Tribes' lands.
covered_parties: Individuals, corporations, agencies, and public or private institutions seeking to conduct studies, surveys, or research projects on the Tribes' lands when the work was not specifically requested or contracted for by the Tribes; Tribal employees also have a records-nondisclosure duty.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction; no Nation-attributed claim is confirmed by the Nation. Current-status assessment is based on the official published code chapter and its legislative-history statement.

claims.claim: Any individual, corporation, agency, or public or private institution wishing to undertake a study, survey, or research project on the Tribes' lands must first obtain a permit approved by the Tribal Council or its designate.
claims.conditions: Applies when the project was not specifically requested or contracted for by the Tribes.

claims.claim: Before a permit may issue, the applicant must execute a written agreement with the Tribes governing how the project will be conducted and creating a fiduciary relationship.
claims.conditions: Applies when the project was not specifically requested or contracted for by the Tribes.

claims.claim: The required written agreement must limit the researcher's right to publish results or conclusions until the Tribes have an opportunity to review and comment, and the final published product must include comments officially made by or for the Tribes.
claims.conditions: Applies to the agreement required for a project not specifically requested or contracted for by the Tribes; the provision requires review and inclusion of comments, not express Tribal approval of publication.

claims.claim: Before a permit may issue, the applicant must provide for approval the project's purpose and profit status, funding source and amount, methodology, participants, dates, information sources, intended final product and publication plan, intended use of results, and steps protecting Tribal and member rights.
claims.conditions: Approval is by the Tribal Council chair or designate; a performance bond is additionally required only when the Tribes deem it appropriate.

claims.claim: The Tribes may cancel a permit if the project is or has been conducted contrary to the approved study design or the agreement supporting the permit.
claims.conditions: Cancellation is conditioned on an apparent deviation from the approved design or underlying agreement.

claims.claim: The Tribes may refuse a permit when they determine that there is insufficient time for appropriate Tribal review, evaluation, supervision, or participation.
claims.conditions: Applies when the Tribes deem review time insufficient.

claims.claim: The Tribes may charge a permit fee when the project is conducted for profit or its results or publication are intended or likely to financially benefit the researcher or research organization.
claims.conditions: Limited to for-profit projects or projects whose results or publication are intended or likely to produce financial benefit.

claims.claim: The researcher must furnish the Tribal Chair's office, without charge, at least one copy of every interim or progress report and the final report resulting from the project.
claims.conditions: Applies to reports resulting from a study, survey, or research project governed by the chapter.

claims.claim: Tribal employees must not provide information from Tribal files or records to a person or organization conducting research that is not authorized under the chapter.
claims.conditions: The nondisclosure duty applies when the recipient's research is not authorized under Chapter 1-10; violation exposes the employee to censure or dismissal.

claims.claim: A person attempting research that was neither specifically requested or contracted for by the Tribes nor permitted under the chapter is subject to available civil remedies under Tribal and Oregon law.
claims.conditions: Applies only to research outside both stated authorization paths.

title: Research Regulation (Tribal Code Chapter 1-10)

tsdf_mapping.notes: The permit-and-written-agreement gate partly resembles T2 negotiated sharing, and the unauthorized-records restriction resembles sovereign control; publication-comment rights, fiduciary duties, fees, report return, and remedies are not represented by tier behavior alone.

covered_data: Research project proposals, applications, contracts, permits, progress and result reports, all project data and work product, documents, correspondence, specimens, raw materials, Tribal Intellectual Property, and information affecting the Tribe, Tribal lands, or Tribal members.
covered_parties: All persons subject to the Tribe's civil jurisdiction who undertake research directly affecting the Tribe or Tribal members, and all researchers conducting projects on Tribal lands or directly affecting the Tribe, Tribal lands, or Tribal members, including requested, sponsored, independent, Tribal, state, and federal researchers; defined minor projects receive limited exceptions.
notes: DRAFT machine extraction; no Nation-attributed claim is confirmed by the Nation. Modal and exception language follows the version amended through 2025-02-20.

claims.claim: A person undertaking research directly affecting the Tribe or Tribal members is deemed to have consented to the legislative and regulatory jurisdiction of the Tribal Council and the adjudicatory jurisdiction of the Tribal Court.
claims.conditions: Applies to research activities directly affecting the Tribe or Tribal members and is bounded by the ordinance's territorial and civil-jurisdiction scope.

claims.claim: A researcher must obtain a Tribal Council-approved permit before undertaking a project on Tribal lands or directly affecting the Tribe, Tribal lands, or Tribal members.
claims.conditions: Extends to research requested or sponsored by Tribal bodies and to independent and Tribal, state, or federal researchers, subject to the minor-project exception in § 9.104.

claims.claim: A minor-project researcher must complete a Research Permit application if the project later ceases to qualify as minor.
claims.conditions: The initial exemption applies only when the project will not invade Tribal members' privacy and will not be published or made publicly available.

claims.claim: A minor-project researcher must execute a waiver when the Tribe's chief executive determines that a waiver is necessary.
claims.conditions: The researcher may proceed before the executed waiver is received only after orally agreeing to execute it.

claims.claim: By executing the minor-project waiver, the researcher agrees not to invade Tribal members' privacy and not to publish or make the project publicly available without the Tribe's written permission.
claims.conditions: Applies to a minor project for which the waiver is required; a student must attach the waiver to submitted work or otherwise prevent publication without the student's knowledge or Tribal permission.

claims.claim: The Tribe may require the researcher to pay or contribute to fees and expenses for an outside consultant needed to review a research proposal.
claims.conditions: Applies when the research clearinghouse needs outside expertise and the Tribe must hire a consultant.

claims.claim: A prospective researcher must complete the Tribe's application materials and appear at the Tribal Council meeting at which the proposal and clearinghouse recommendations are presented.
claims.conditions: Applies to projects that require a research permit under the ordinance.

claims.claim: Researchers must provide for Tribal oversight and report regularly to the Tribal Council and liaison department on project progress and results.
claims.conditions: Applies to permitted research projects governed by the ordinance.

claims.claim: The Tribe may retain sole ownership of all data, documents, correspondence, reports, specimens, and other information or items produced, generated, or gathered by the researcher.
claims.conditions: The Research Contract may make an express exception to sole Tribal ownership.

claims.claim: The Tribe may withdraw consent to use or release information and may prevent publication of data that is unauthorized, insensitive, misrepresents or stereotypes Tribal people, or will harm the Tribe, its health, safety, welfare, or environment.
claims.conditions: Exercise is limited to the stated unauthorized, insensitive, misrepresentative, stereotyping, or harmful uses or publications.

claims.claim: If the Tribe withdraws approval of a research project, the researcher must return all information, data, raw materials, and specimens to the Tribe.
claims.conditions: Triggered only when the Tribe withdraws project approval.

claims.claim: A researcher must not use work product generated under a Research Contract or Permit for another purpose without the Tribal Council's express written consent.
claims.conditions: Applies to secondary use of work product generated under the Research Contract or Permit.

claims.claim: For violations, the Tribal Court may grant injunctive relief, order preservation and return of property including data, forfeit a performance bond, order restitution, impose civil penalties up to $5,000, and grant other relief needed to redress injury.
claims.conditions: Available in an action brought for violation of the ordinance; separate provisions also authorize removal, civil suit, and affiliation consequences.

claims.claim: Tribal Administration must adopt policies for retaining, managing, and destroying records in its custody and must comply with applicable federal and Oregon records-retention requirements.
claims.conditions: Policy development must consider records' cultural, historical, legal, administrative, and research value.

claims.claim: Only Tribal members and Tribal newspaper staff acting in their official capacity have a right under the ordinance to obtain copies of covered records.
claims.conditions: The right remains subject to the ordinance's unavailable-record categories, request procedures, and department-policy limits.

claims.claim: Tribal newspaper staff may use records obtained under the ordinance only in connection with their official newspaper work.
claims.conditions: Applies to copies obtained by newspaper staff under the ordinance.

claims.claim: Records in the ordinance's unavailable categories must not be released to the membership except through the category-specific exceptions stated by the ordinance.
claims.conditions: Categories include records confidential under law or policy; investigatory audits; security reports; land transactions; confidentiality restrictions or court orders; privileged or work-product material; records whose disclosure may adversely affect Tribal political or business strategies; and investment directives, strategies, and holdings. Some categories permit release by the identified Tribal authority, and personal records may be available to the member they identify.

claims.claim: When a record contains both available and exempt information, the Records Administrator must separate the information and provide the nonexempt portion through redaction or another method that makes the withholding apparent.
claims.conditions: Applies only to mixed records containing both information available under the ordinance and information exempt from disclosure.

claims.claim: Supplemental information submitted by a Tribal member to address allegedly false or misleading information must be kept with the applicable record.
claims.conditions: The member must be identified in the record and submit the required written summary and additional information to the appropriate Tribal offices.

claims.claim: A Tribal official or employee must not provide a record or information from it to a person who is not authorized to have access under the ordinance.
claims.conditions: Disclosure is permitted when it is in the ordinary course of the official's or employee's position or is authorized by Tribal Council.

claims.claim: A person must not knowingly possess or distribute a record, or information contained in a record, that the person is not authorized to possess or distribute.
claims.conditions: The violation requires knowing possession or distribution without authorization.

claims.claim: A person found by the Tribal Court to have violated the ordinance is subject to escalating civil fines for the first three violations and loss of the right to view or obtain record copies after a fourth violation.
claims.conditions: Sanctions depend on a Tribal Court finding and the number of prior violations.

covered_data: Research involving or about NWIC faculty, staff, and students, plus research accepted for NWIC IRB review at a Tribe, TCU, or TBO's request; research data, biological and non-biological materials, analyses, presentations, reports, publications, and consent documents.
covered_parties: The Northwest Indian College IRB; researchers and institutions whose research is reviewed by that IRB; and participating Tribes, Tribal colleges and universities, and Tribal-based organizations only when they ask the NWIC IRB to review research or otherwise participate under the policy.
notes: DRAFT machine extraction. Northwest Indian College is the issuer. The policy names several Nations and organizations as served by its IRB, but no Nation linkage or adoption is confirmed here; any such applicability requires human and Nation review. The fetched policy also says it will be reviewed every three years, so current-version status should be checked.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must comply with 45 CFR part 46 and NWIC's Tribal-self-determined components and criteria.
claims.conditions: The policy's scope is the NWIC IRB.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must review all research by or about NWIC faculty, staff, and students and research that Tribes, TCUs, or TBOs ask it to review.
claims.conditions: The second branch applies only when a Tribe, TCU, or TBO asks the NWIC IRB to conduct the review.

claims.claim: The NWIC President must appoint all IRB members, and the IRB must have at least nine members, including at least two federally recognized Tribal descendants with primary nonscientific expertise.
claims.conditions: The policy assigns highest priority to Elders interested in reviewing research.

claims.claim: At least one federally recognized Tribal descendant with primary nonscientific expertise must participate in every full and expedited review.
The NWIC IRB must review all research within the policy's Tribal-self-determined definition.
claims.conditions: The policy's definition includes research involving people not living or identifiable and Tribes, TCUs, or TBOs, and includes some public-health investigations and oral histories.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must review federally exempt research using its expedited procedure.
claims.conditions: The policy explains that federal exempt categories did not consider potential community harms.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must require informed consent from participating Tribes, TCUs, and TBOs, not only from individual participants.
claims.conditions: Applies to research reviewed under the policy when those entities participate.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must review and approve, require modification of, or disapprove all proposed presentations, research reports, and publications.
claims.conditions: The review is to minimize harms and risks, maximize benefits, and recognize self-determination and Tribal sovereignty for participating Tribes, TCUs, and TBOs.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must give full review to research falling within the federal expedited-review categories.
claims.conditions: The policy states that the federal expedited list did not consider potential community harms.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must apply criteria under which each participating Tribe, TCU, or TBO owns and controls data, biological and non-biological materials, and analyses about itself and its members and determines access to them.
claims.conditions: The criteria use legally effective terms approved by Tribal-based attorneys and do not apply when the participating Tribe, TCU, or TBO defers.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB may require a researcher's institution to sign a legal Data and Material Sharing and Ownership Agreement with each participating Tribe, TCU, or TBO.
claims.conditions: The agreements contain ownership, control, and access provisions; the requirement does not apply when the participating Tribe, TCU, or TBO defers.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must report all serious problems and responses to participating Tribes, TCUs, and TBOs and consult their experts before responding to an appeal of an IRB decision.
claims.conditions: Applies to the Tribes, TCUs, and TBOs participating in the research.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must review cooperative research and must not approve it when NWIC IRB conditions are unmet.
claims.conditions: Nonapproval does not apply when the participating Tribes, TCUs, or TBOs defer.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must ensure that informed-consent documents contain all Tribal-self-determined benefits, protections, and conditions stated in any Data and Material Sharing and Ownership Agreement.
claims.conditions: Applies when a DMSOA exists.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must periodically report its activities to the NWIC President and to every Tribe, TCU, and TBO for which it reviews research.
claims.conditions: Reporting is limited to entities for which the NWIC IRB conducts research review.

claims.claim: Guides must not guide clients to archaeological or other sensitive cultural-resource sites, and guides and outfitters must not disclose archaeological-site information to clients.
claims.conditions: Applies to guides and outfitters operating under Chapter 3-2.

claims.cite: Section 3-3-7(f), Water Rights Administration p. 4 / PDF p. 35
claims.claim: The Water Resources Division may coordinate with the Idaho Department of Water Resources by sharing and exchanging water-use information and acquiring the state agency's database of state-administered water rights and permits within the Reservation.
claims.conditions: Only as necessary to administer the chapter and Tribal water rights.

claims.cite: Section 3-3-10(c), Water Rights Administration p. 5 / PDF p. 36
claims.claim: The Water Resources Division must establish and maintain a geodatabase of existing Tribal water rights, permitted existing uses, new-use permits, and later-acquired Tribal water rights.
claims.conditions: Applies to the categories enumerated in the section.

claims.cite: Section 3-3-11(c)-(e), Water Rights Administration pp. 6-7 / PDF pp. 37-38
claims.claim: Water-permit applications must provide at least applicant contact information and the proposed source, diversion point and quantity, works, place and purpose of use, and use period; issued permits must state corresponding limitations and inspection, metering, and compliance conditions.
claims.conditions: Application and permit content is subject to Section 3-3-11 and its stated exceptions from the permit requirement.

claims.cite: Section 3-3-11(f), Water Rights Administration p. 7 / PDF p. 38
claims.claim: Every issued water permit must be publicly noticed: an abstract must run for three weeks in a newspaper of general circulation within the Reservation and the permit itself must be posted for three weeks in Tribal and BIA offices and other appropriate locations.
claims.conditions: Applies to permits for both existing and new uses.

claims.cite: Section 3-3-13(f), Water Rights Administration p. 10 / PDF p. 41
claims.claim: Permit-decision hearings must be recorded, and an applicant or other affected party may request a transcript at that party's own expense if an appeal is sought.
claims.conditions: Transcript access is conditioned on an appeal and payment by the requesting affected party.

claims.cite: Section 3-3-15(e), Water Rights Administration p. 11 / PDF p. 42
claims.claim: On notice of an appeal, the Water Rights Administration Commission must certify and transmit the administrative record to the Clerk of the Tribal Court of Appeals within fourteen calendar days, including the documents, transcripts, and other information supporting the appealed decision.
claims.conditions: Triggered by Commission receipt of notice that an appeal has been filed.

title: Nez Perce Tribe Revised Code - Title 3: Natural Resources and Environment

tsdf_mapping.notes: The title mixes public notices, regulated partner reporting, interagency exchange, confidential cultural-site information, and sovereign water-rights records. TSDF can express broad release or control posture, but not the title's role-, purpose-, timing-, subject-matter-, and remedy-specific obligations.

covered_data: Enrollment records and supporting vital or family documents; gaming licensing, background, financial, audit, and personnel records; business and tax transaction reports; public meeting reports and rules; hemp contact, land, geospatial, production, disposal, testing, enforcement, and transfer records.
covered_parties: Enrollment applicants and members; the Bureau of Indian Affairs; NPTEC and enrollment staff; Gaming Commission members and staff, gaming operations, employees, license applicants and licensees; fuel, tobacco, liquor, and retail enterprises; the Tribal Athletic Commission; hemp applicants, producers, licensees, laboratories, the Agriculture Office, USDA, and law-enforcement bodies.
notes: DRAFT machine extraction from the official Revised Code compilation, which labels the Gaming Ordinance amended through Resolution NP 23-482 on September 26, 2023. No Nation-attributed claim is confirmed without Nation review.

claims.cite: Section 6-1-7(a), Enrollment Ordinance p. 6 / PDF p. 15
claims.claim: Official Nez Perce enrollment records must be maintained by the Bureau of Indian Affairs as directed by official NPTEC actions.
claims.conditions: Applies to administration of the Tribe's official enrollment records.

claims.cite: Section 6-1-7(c)-(d), Enrollment Ordinance p. 6 / PDF p. 15
claims.claim: An enrollee requesting a status-based change to enrollment records must provide the Enrollment Committee with the official and supporting documents specified by the ordinance, after which the Committee must review the request and submit a recommendation to NPTEC.
claims.conditions: Applies to record changes caused by a change in enrollee status, not mere omission or misprint corrections.

claims.cite: Section 6.20.007(I), Gaming Ordinance p. 7 / PDF p. 24
claims.claim: Current and former Gaming Commission members must keep licensing-decision information and communications confidential, except that they may release them to law enforcement to further an investigation or otherwise comply with law.
claims.conditions: Disclosure exception is limited to law-enforcement investigation or other legal compliance.

claims.cite: Section 6.20.008(A)(6)-(8), Gaming Ordinance p. 7 / PDF p. 24
claims.claim: The Gaming Commission must ensure proper records of gambling proceeds and gaming activity, review suitability-related licensee records at least every three years, conduct annual independent Class II and III audits, and submit audit results to NIGC within 120 days after each fiscal year.
claims.conditions: Audit scope includes specified gaming contracts of at least $25,000, except professional legal and accounting contracts, and must conform to generally accepted auditing standards.

claims.cite: Section 6.20.013(F), Gaming Ordinance p. 10 / PDF p. 27
claims.claim: Gaming operations and employees must provide the Gaming Commission required reports, audits, and service or supply contract results at least quarterly or sooner when required.
claims.conditions: Applies to reports, audits, and contract results required by the Gaming Commission.

claims.cite: Section 6.20.014(B), Gaming Ordinance p. 12 / PDF p. 29
claims.claim: At reasonable intervals set by the Gaming Commission, gaming facilities must provide a sworn report detailing all receipts and disbursements and other reasonably required compliance information.
claims.conditions: Timing and additional information are set by the Commission and must relate to compliance with the ordinance and other applicable law.

claims.cite: Section 6.20.018(B)-(D), Gaming Ordinance pp. 13-14 / PDF pp. 30-31
claims.claim: Gaming license application forms for key employees and primary management officials must display the prescribed Privacy Act notice before completion, explaining official uses, authorized regulatory or law-enforcement disclosures, and that withholding consent prevents licensing.
claims.conditions: Applies to the specified individual gaming-license forms; social-security-number disclosure is stated as voluntary though omission may cause processing errors.

claims.cite: Section 6.20.020(D), Gaming Ordinance p. 17 / PDF p. 34
claims.claim: The Gaming Commission and its background investigators must keep confidential the identity of each person interviewed during a background investigation.
claims.conditions: Applies to interviewee identities obtained in licensing background investigations.

claims.cite: Section 6.20.020(E)-(F), Gaming Ordinance p. 17 / PDF p. 34
claims.claim: FBI criminal-history information received from NIGC may be reviewed only by the Gaming Commission and background investigator, and no further dissemination is authorized.
claims.conditions: Also subject to the Tribe's identified memorandum of understanding with NIGC.

claims.cite: Section 6.20.022, Gaming Ordinance pp. 17-18 / PDF pp. 34-35
claims.claim: Before issuing a permanent license, the Gaming Commission must prepare and submit to NIGC a Notice of Results containing the specified applicant identifiers, work date, licensing and criminal-history summary, and eligibility determination no later than sixty days after work begins.
claims.conditions: Applies to primary management official and key employee permanent licenses.

claims.cite: Section 6.20.026, Gaming Ordinance pp. 22-23 / PDF pp. 39-40
claims.claim: The Gaming Commission must maintain a complete application file while a primary management official or key employee is employed and retain licensing applications, investigation reports, and eligibility determinations for at least three years after termination.
claims.conditions: The complete file contains the information listed in Section 6.20.019(A); the three-year floor runs from employment termination.

claims.cite: Section 6-6-4(c), Fuel Tax Ordinance pp. 1-2 / PDF pp. 56-57
claims.claim: Every fuel distributor, licensed or not, must submit by the tenth day of the following month a report of fuel received and other information reasonably requested by the administrator.
claims.conditions: If the tenth falls on a weekend or holiday, the report is due the next business day.

claims.cite: Section 6-6-5(c)-(d), Fuel Tax Ordinance p. 2 / PDF p. 57
claims.claim: Every fuel retailer, licensed or not, must submit by the tenth day of the following month a report of fuel received and sold, reasonably requested additional information, and the associated tax remittance.
claims.conditions: If the tenth falls on a weekend or holiday, the report is due the next business day.

claims.cite: Sections 6-7-2(a),(c)-(d), Tobacco and Liquor Ordinance pp. 1-2 / PDF pp. 60-61
claims.claim: All tobacco wholesalers and retailers must report every Reservation tobacco transaction, including purchaser identity and contact information; licensed distributors must also file complete monthly sales reports by the prescribed postmark deadline, including quantities and recipient names and addresses.
claims.conditions: Applies whether or not the wholesaler or retailer is licensed; a no-sales month still requires a report from a licensed distributor.

claims.cite: Section 6-7-3(d),(f)-(g), Tobacco and Liquor Ordinance pp. 3-4 / PDF pp. 62-63
claims.claim: Each tobacco outlet must report the prior month's purchase volume to the Tribe by the tenth day of each month and provide full and accurate information on applications and monthly reports plus reasonably requested information.
claims.conditions: Late or missing reports trigger the ordinance's presumptions and sanctions.

claims.cite: Section 6-7-4(d),(f)-(g), Tobacco and Liquor Ordinance pp. 5-6 / PDF pp. 64-65
claims.claim: Each liquor distributor or outlet must report the prior month's liquor-purchase volume to the Tribe by the tenth day of each month and provide full and accurate application and monthly-report information plus reasonably requested information.
claims.conditions: Late or missing reports trigger the ordinance's presumptions and sanctions.

claims.cite: Sections 6-9-6(d) and 6-9-7, Athletic Commission p. 2 / PDF p. 76
claims.claim: The Tribal Athletic Commission's secretary-treasurer must timely compile and publish written public-meeting reports; all public-meeting reports and approved rules and regulations must be available to the public, with executive-session exceptions for specified confidential matters.
claims.conditions: Public access does not extend to executive sessions validly convened for personnel, contract, litigation, or confidential information matters.

claims.cite: Section 6-11-4, Sales Tax Ordinance p. 1 / PDF p. 90
claims.claim: Each tribally owned retail enterprise must submit monthly reports of taxed sales to the Nez Perce Tribe Department of Finance by the tenth day of the following month.
claims.conditions: If the tenth falls on a weekend or holiday, the report is due the next business day.

claims.cite: Section 6-13-40, Hemp Regulatory Code p. 15 / PDF p. 106
claims.claim: Hemp licensees must provide annual harvest, contact, and disposal reports containing the information required by the Agriculture Office, and the Agriculture Office must share required information with USDA Agricultural Marketing Service through the federal hemp platform.
claims.conditions: Additional report information must be considered necessary by the Agriculture Office and consented to in the license application; USDA sharing is limited to what is required.

claims.cite: Section 6-13-41, Hemp Regulatory Code p. 15 / PDF p. 106
claims.claim: The Agriculture Office must retain hemp licenses, applications, USDA reports, relevant registered-land-area information, and land legal descriptions for at least three calendar years.
claims.conditions: Applies to every approved hemp-production site.

claims.cite: Section 6-13-42, Hemp Regulatory Code p. 15 / PDF p. 106
claims.claim: Except for required USDA reporting and disclosures to law enforcement, the Agriculture Office must remove listed personally identifiable information, including names, addresses, government identifiers, GPS coordinates, telephone numbers, and email addresses, and shield it to the maximum extent permitted by law.
claims.conditions: USDA reporting and law-enforcement disclosures are express exceptions.

claims.cite: Section 6-13-43(a)-(b), Hemp Regulatory Code pp. 15-16 / PDF pp. 106-107
claims.claim: The Agriculture Office must submit monthly producer and disposal reports and an annual acreage report to USDA in the specified federal platform format, containing the enumerated producer, land, acreage, license, status, disposal, and testing information.
claims.conditions: Monthly disposal details apply only when disposals occurred; the annual report is due December 15.

claims.cite: Section 6-13-43(c), Hemp Regulatory Code pp. 16-17 / PDF pp. 107-108
claims.claim: A licensed hemp producer must submit specified site, acreage, production, and license information to the federal hemp platform and update both FSA and the Tribal Agriculture Office within thirty days after that information changes.
claims.conditions: Applies after receipt of a Nez Perce Tribe hemp license and separately for each production location or license.

claims.cite: Section 6-13-43(d)-(e), Hemp Regulatory Code p. 17 / PDF p. 108
claims.claim: A laboratory conducting the final pre-harvest compliance test must report every test result, passing or failing, to the USDA hemp platform; seasonal monitoring tests not used for pre-harvest compliance are exempt.
claims.conditions: Only the final compliance test is subject to this reporting rule; monitoring tests are expressly excluded.

claims.cite: Section 6-13-44, Hemp Regulatory Code pp. 17-18 / PDF pp. 108-109
claims.claim: Hemp producers and licensees must timely report contact changes, submit planting and pre-harvest reports with specified geospatial, acreage, disposition, date, and location information, and retain sampling and testing documentation for at least three years for production to the Agriculture Office on request.
claims.conditions: Contact and planting reports are due within fourteen days of the triggering event; the harvest report is due at least thirty days before harvest, subject to the stated male-plant exception.

claims.cite: Section 6-13-56, Hemp Regulatory Code p. 22 / PDF p. 113
claims.claim: The Agriculture Office must promptly notify USDA's Agricultural Marketing Service administrator of noncompliant cannabis plants or material and attach records demonstrating proper disposal of the affected harvest lot.
claims.conditions: Triggered when plants or material do not meet the Code's definition of hemp.

claims.cite: Section 6-13-63(a), Hemp Regulatory Code p. 26 / PDF p. 117
claims.claim: If the Agriculture Office determines that a licensee violated Tribal hemp law with a culpable mental state greater than negligence, it must immediately report the licensee to the United States Attorney General or designee and the Nez Perce Tribal Police.
claims.conditions: Requires an Agriculture Office determination of a violation with culpability greater than negligence.

claims.cite: Section 6-13-69, Hemp Regulatory Code p. 27 / PDF p. 118
claims.claim: Every hemp transfer, sale, purchase, or receipt must be documented; sellers must convey license and compliant test-result copies and record transaction details, while purchasers must receive those copies and record available purchase details.
claims.conditions: Applies to hemp transactions governed by Section 6-13-69.

title: Nez Perce Tribe Revised Code - Title 6: Intergovernmental Rules and Regulations

tsdf_mapping.notes: Title 6 contains public records, regulated external disclosures, high-sensitivity enrollment and background records, PII-removal rules, mandatory federal reporting, and transaction records. TSDF tiers can express broad sharing posture but not the title's consent notices, statutory exceptions, retention periods, report schedules, recipient-specific duties, or enforcement triggers.

covered_data: Water-resource studies and data; permit applications and decisions; applicant and property information; geographic, hydrologic, meter, withdrawal, and diversion data; program comments and investigations; well-construction notices; administrative appeal records; and public versus nonpublic Water Code files.
covered_parties: The Roads, Irrigation and Land Committee; Water Code Director and staff; water-use and hydraulic-permit applicants and permittees; well owners and drillers; affected Yakama Nation programs; the Bureau of Indian Affairs and Wapato Irrigation Project; other governmental agencies; and persons requesting public records.
issuing_entity: Confederated Tribes and Bands of the Yakama Nation
notes: DRAFT machine extraction of a resource-management code. It does contain partner-facing and public data obligations, so none_reason is not appropriate. The version notes amendment and supersession by T-027-22; no Nation-attributed claim is confirmed without Nation review.

claims.claim: Consistent with existing Tribal Council and General Council resolutions, the Committee may exchange information and cooperate with other governmental agencies and recommend administrative agreements for appropriate purposes.
claims.conditions: Information exchange and cooperation must be consistent with existing Tribal Council and General Council resolutions.

claims.claim: The Water Code Director and staff must collect and maintain statistical records concerning permit applications, issued permits, and other administrative activities under the Code.
claims.conditions: Applies as an administrative duty under the Code.

claims.claim: The Director and staff must collect, organize, and catalogue existing Reservation-water information and studies from all sources, develop additional data as necessary, solicit comment and expert advice when appropriate, and prepare and disseminate public water-resource information and education materials.
claims.conditions: Additional data development is limited to what is necessary for Code objectives; public comment and expert advice apply when appropriate.

claims.claim: On receiving a completed application for specified water-use permit classes, the Director must publish an online public notice soliciting comment and identifying the applicant, property, proposed use, Water Code Administration contact, and comment deadline.
claims.conditions: Applies to Class 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 9, or 10 applications, subject to the exemptions in Section 60.09.21.

claims.claim: The Director must circulate all water-use permit applications to affected Yakama Nation programs and interested agencies, including the Bureau of Indian Affairs and Wapato Irrigation Project; the recipients must evaluate and comment within fourteen days, and the Director must record the information obtained through review and investigation.
claims.conditions: The inter-disciplinary review path applies at the Director's discretion when a program objects.

claims.claim: Water-use permits must require every Reservation and off-Reservation trust-land diversion and withdrawal to be metered, measured, and reported to Water Code Administration on the timetable set by regulation or permit condition.
claims.conditions: Applies through permit conditions to diversions and withdrawals within the Reservation or on off-Reservation trust land.

claims.cite: Sections 60.13.01 and 60.13.03, code p. 27 / PDF p. 27
claims.claim: Well drillers must collect cuttings and provide information and access to Yakama Nation employees as required by the Director; property owners, agents, or contractors must also give advance written notice containing specified well, owner, use, start-date, and driller information.
claims.conditions: Applies to well construction, alteration, or decommissioning; additional information is limited to what the Director requires.

claims.claim: On receiving a completed hydraulic-permit application, the Director must publish an online public notice soliciting comment and identifying the applicant, property, proposed use, Water Code Administration contact, and comment deadline.
claims.conditions: Applies to the hydraulic-permit process in Section 60.15.05; culturally sensitive area consultation remains internal to Yakama Nation programs.

claims.claim: For an appeal, the Committee must certify and transmit to the Tribal Council clerk the administrative record, including all supporting documents, minutes, transcripts, Director materials, and other decision-basis information, resolving relevance doubts in favor of inclusion.
claims.conditions: Applies to Tribal Council review of a Committee final decision.

claims.claim: The Director and Committee must maintain files for applications, permits, and other Code activities; application and permit copies are open to public inspection and copying at the requester's expense, but the Code does not make other documents public and expressly excludes investigative, draft, internal, privileged, and legal-counsel communications.
claims.conditions: Public access is confined to applications and permits; copying is at the requester's expense and the listed nonpublic categories are nonexclusive.

claims.claim: The Director and Committee must plan for all existing and future Reservation and off-Reservation trust-land diversions and withdrawals to be metered, measured, and reported to Water Code Administration according to a regulatory timetable.
claims.conditions: This is a planning duty addressing existing and future diversions and withdrawals.

title: Title LX (60) - Water Code (2016 Edition V.3.2022)

tsdf_mapping.notes: The Code combines public notices and public applications, intergovernmental information exchange, permit-specific partner reporting, and protected internal or privileged files. TSDF can represent broad release and control classes, but not these recipient-, purpose-, process-, exception-, and record-type-specific legal rules.
